Recent NEWS from ARDES

Recent NEWS from ARDES

A New Era in Academic Incentives and Support

Within the framework of the

Scientific Publications Support and Incentive Program (BEDEK)

implemented by the

Research Support Office (ARDES) was revised and

incentive amounts for

Web of Science–indexed publications

were

doubled

for

single-author articles published in Quartile 1 journals

and for

five-author articles

, compared to 2024 levels.

The scope of the Scientific Publications Support and Incentive Program (BEDEK) was expanded to include

Scopus-indexed publications.

To further leverage the strong publication capacity of academic staff,

132 graduate students

were

fully funded through institutional resources

in the past year.

Addressing institutional needs, the

University Rectorate

supports

postdoctoral employment

through

TÜBİTAK-funded bilateral collaborations

,

Scientific Research (BAP) projects

, and

COST Actions

; admits

adjunct faculty

through international collaborations; and provides

ARDES-supported funding

for

short-term research visits

and

conference participation

.

The improved incentive schemes, a transparent incentive application system for academic staff, and quality- and impact-oriented collaborative publication activities constitute the most visible indicators of these efforts. However, as the Office became operational in the 2025–2026 period, its broader impact will be evaluated over the next five years based on the University’s performance in international rankings and the upward academic performance of its faculty members. For instance, in two reports, we can see an upward trend in academic performance,
